solution_summary: Implement a localized fix in flight search so origin airport filtering becomes case-insensitive by normalizing the incoming origin query before comparison, and add a regression test proving lowercase input matches uppercase-stored airport codes.
implementation_steps: Update `backend/app/services/flight_service.py` in `search_flights` to normalize the `origin` query parameter before applying the filter (for example, compare against an uppercased value or use a case-insensitive comparison).
Keep the change scoped to origin matching so other search filters retain current behavior unless they share the same normalization path.
Add a regression test in `backend/app/tests/test_flight_service.py` that calls `search_flights` with `origin=ath` and verifies it returns the same flights as `origin=ATH` when matching data exists.
If the test suite already has a shared flight fixture/helper, reuse it to avoid duplicating setup and to keep the test focused on the casing regression.

planned_changes.change_summary: Normalize the incoming origin airport code in `search_flights` before matching against stored flight origin codes.
planned_changes.rationale: The bug is caused by case-sensitive comparison against uppercase airport codes; fixing the comparison in the service layer directly addresses the issue with minimal scope.

planned_changes.change_summary: Add a regression test covering lowercase origin input and verifying it matches flights stored with uppercase airport codes.
planned_changes.rationale: This prevents the bug from reappearing and documents the expected case-insensitive behavior.

patch_strategy: Make the smallest possible service-layer change: normalize only the origin search input within `search_flights` and leave other filters untouched. Prefer a targeted comparison fix over broader request parsing or schema changes to avoid side effects. Then add a focused regression test that demonstrates the lowercase-to-uppercase matching behavior and confirms existing uppercase behavior still works. If the service already normalizes other airport-code filters consistently, mirror the same helper/pattern only for origin search to maintain internal consistency without expanding scope.
validation_steps: Run the flight service test module, especially the new regression case in `backend/app/tests/test_flight_service.py`.
Run any existing search/filter tests to confirm uppercase origin queries still behave identically.
Manually verify the endpoint with `GET /flights/search?origin=ath` and `GET /flights/search?origin=ATH` returning the same results for known seeded data.
If there are tests for other flight search filters, confirm they still pass to ensure no unintended normalization changes were introduced.
pr_title: SDLC-63: Fix case-insensitive origin flight search
pr_body:
## Summary
Fix flight origin search so lowercase airport codes match uppercase-stored airport codes.

## What changed
- Normalize origin input inside `search_flights`
- Add a regression test for lowercase origin queries

## Why
`GET /flights/search?origin=ath` was returning no results even when flights existed for `ATH`.

## Validation
- Added regression coverage for lowercase origin input
- Confirmed uppercase origin search remains supported
- Verified the change is localized to flight search origin matching
risks: If other airport-code filters use similar case-sensitive comparisons, they may still exhibit the same bug until separately addressed.

If the service currently relies on exact string matching for any downstream logic tied to `origin`, normalization should be limited to the filter comparison only to avoid altering stored data or response payloads.
assumptions: `search_flights` is the single place where origin filtering is applied for `/flights/search`.
Airport codes in persisted flight records are consistently stored in uppercase, as stated in the issue context.
The existing test suite has a straightforward way to construct or seed flights for service-level search tests.
